 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-zwg3","title":"TASK: Add final completion certificate to onboard","description":"# TASK: Add final completion certificate to onboard

## Context
When user completes all 9 lessons, show a special completion screen.

## Proposed Screen
```
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                             │
│  🏆 ACFS ONBOARDING COMPLETE!                               │
│                                                             │
│  Congratulations! You've completed all 9 lessons.           │
│                                                             │
│  You're now ready to:                                       │
│  • Launch AI agents with cc, cod, gmi                       │
│  • Manage sessions with ntm                                 │
│  • Search code with rg                                      │
│  • Use the full flywheel workflow                           │
│                                                             │
│  Next steps:                                                │
│  • Run 'acfs info' for quick reference                      │
│  • Run 'acfs cheatsheet' for all commands                   │
│  • Start a project with 'ntm new myproject && cc'           │
│                                                             │
│  Completed: $(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')                       │
│                                                             │
│  Happy coding! 🚀                                            │
│                                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Implementation

### Store Completion Timestamp
```bash
# In onboard_progress.json
{
  "completed_lessons": [0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8],
  "completed_at": "2025-01-15T14:30:00Z"
}
```

### Trigger Condition
Show when:
- User just completed lesson 8 (last)
- OR user runs `onboard` when all lessons complete

### Re-access
User can re-run `onboard` to see certificate again.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Certificate shown on final completion
- [ ] Completion timestamp stored
- [ ] Shows next steps recommendations
- [ ] Accessible by running onboard again

Shows trophy option when all 9 lessons complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-zwg3","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kyhv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T20:00:16.782605Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-10sv","title":"Fix SLB installation failure on Ubuntu 25.04","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:38:30.188603390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T14:42:35.650533697Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T14:42:35.650514952Z","close_reason":"Fixed by installing SLB via .deb (commit 75a91c50)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-12kj","title":"Deep exploration: WA (WezTerm Automata)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of WA (Web Agent / WebView Automation) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify WA installation (check for common web automation paths)
[[ -d /dp/web_agent ]] && echo "PASS: wa repo exists" || echo "INFO: wa repo path differs"
command -v wa &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: wa command available" || echo "INFO: wa not in PATH"

# Check browser automation dependencies
command -v playwright &>/dev/null && echo "INFO: playwright available" || echo "INFO: playwright not installed"
command -v chromium &>/dev/null && echo "INFO: chromium available" || echo "INFO: chromium not installed"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/wa-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- Find WA repo and read README
- Check for browser automation docs, screenshot capabilities

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Browser automation mechanism (Playwright? Puppeteer?)
  - Screenshot capture capabilities
  - Form filling and interaction
  - Integration with Claude chrome extension
  - Headless vs headed mode

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
wa --help 2>&1 | head -20 || echo "No wa command"
# Check for alternative names
browser-agent --help 2>&1 | head -10 || echo "No browser-agent"
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'web agent OR browser automation OR playwright'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'wa browser screenshot' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Find WA repo location
- Review recent commits

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Browser automation engine
- [ ] Screenshot capabilities
- [ ] Form interaction
- [ ] Claude extension integration
- [ ] Headless operation

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] claude-chrome - Chrome extension
- [ ] mail - screenshot sharing
- [ ] ntm - coordinated browser tasks

### 2.3 Capability Verification
```bash
# Check for playwright installation
npx playwright --version 2>/dev/null || echo "playwright not installed"

# Check for browser binaries
ls ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ 2>/dev/null | head -5 || echo "No cached browsers"
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update wa entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Browser automation
- `description`: Web interaction capabilities
- `deepDescription`: How automation works
- `features`: Verified capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test wa entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const wa = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'wa');
console.log('Testing wa entry...');
console.assert(wa, 'wa entry exists');
console.assert(wa.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Browser Automation (Safe Mode)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/wa-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== WA E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: Help/availability
echo "Test 1: Help check..." | tee -a $LOG
wa --help 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "wa not available"

# Test 2: Screenshot capability
echo "Test 2: Screenshot help..." | tee -a $LOG
wa screenshot --help 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No screenshot command"

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-12kj --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Browser capabilities documented correctly
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:02:06.153300827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:38:10.013400309Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:38:10.013370132Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-144la","title":"Add opt-in agent notification system via ntfy.sh","description":"## Problem

Users running agents on remote VPS have no way to get notified when agents complete, fail, or need attention, without actively watching terminals. Ref: GH issue #131.

## Solution

Opt-in notification system using ntfy.sh -- zero-config by default, one-line enable, push to any device via HTTP pub/sub. Builds on the existing webhook.sh pattern.

## Architecture

### How ntfy.sh Works
- Topic-based HTTP pub/sub: curl -d "message" ntfy.sh/my-topic
- No signup, no API keys needed for public server
- Topic name serves as de facto password (choose unguessable names)
- Self-hosted option: same API, different server URL
- Clients: Android, iOS, web, CLI, or any HTTP client
- Priority levels: min, low, default, high, urgent

### Configuration Storage
Location: ~/.config/acfs/config.yaml (XDG-compliant, same as webhook_url)

New keys:
  notifications:
    enabled: false               # Master switch (default: off)
    server: "https://ntfy.sh"    # Default public server, or self-hosted URL
    topic: ""                    # Auto-generated on enable (e.g., acfs-{hostname}-{random8})
    events:                      # Which events trigger notifications
      agent_session_end: true    # Agent session completed/crashed
      install_complete: true     # ACFS install/update finished
      ci_failure: true           # GH Actions workflow failed
      doctor_failure: true       # acfs doctor found issues
      nightly_update: false      # Nightly update results (noisy, default off)
    priority:
      default: "default"        # Normal events
      failure: "high"           # Failures get high priority
      crash: "urgent"           # Agent crashes get urgent priority
    quiet_hours:
      enabled: false
      start: "23:00"
      end: "07:00"

### Subcommand: acfs notifications

Add to acfs.zshrc dispatcher and create scripts/lib/notifications.sh:

  acfs notifications enable     # Generate topic, enable, show subscribe URL
  acfs notifications disable    # Turn off
  acfs notifications test       # Send test notification
  acfs notifications status     # Show current config
  acfs notifications topic      # Print the subscribe URL for easy copying
  acfs notifications events     # List configurable events
  acfs notifications set-server <url>  # Switch to self-hosted

### Enable Flow
1. acfs notifications enable
2. Generate random topic: acfs-$(hostname)-$(openssl rand -hex 4) e.g. acfs-devbox-a1b2c3d4
3. Write to ~/.config/acfs/config.yaml
4. Print: "Notifications enabled! Subscribe on your devices:"
5. Print: "  Web: https://ntfy.sh/acfs-devbox-a1b2c3d4"
6. Print: "  App: Open ntfy app, add topic: acfs-devbox-a1b2c3d4"
7. Send test notification: "ACFS notifications enabled on $(hostname)"

### Notification Library: scripts/lib/notify.sh
Follows the same pattern as scripts/lib/webhook.sh:
- Source with: source ~/.acfs/scripts/lib/notify.sh
- Function: acfs_notify(title, message, priority, tags)
- Non-blocking: background curl with 5s timeout, disowned
- Best-effort: failures are silently ignored (notifications should never block workflows)
- HTTPS-only validation on server URL
- Respects quiet_hours config
- Logs to ~/.acfs/logs/notifications.log (append-only, one line per notification with timestamp)

Implementation:
  acfs_notify() {
    local title="$1" message="$2" priority="${3:-default}" tags="${4:-}"
    local config_file="$HOME/.config/acfs/config.yaml"
    # Check enabled
    local enabled; enabled=$(yq -r '.notifications.enabled // "false"' "$config_file" 2>/dev/null)
    [ "$enabled" = "true" ] || return 0
    # Read config
    local server; server=$(yq -r '.notifications.server // "https://ntfy.sh"' "$config_file")
    local topic; topic=$(yq -r '.notifications.topic // ""' "$config_file")
    [ -n "$topic" ] || return 0
    # Check quiet hours
    if _acfs_in_quiet_hours; then return 0; fi
    # Send (non-blocking)
    curl -sf -o /dev/null \
      -H "Title: $title" \
      -H "Priority: $priority" \
      ${tags:+-H "Tags: $tags"} \
      -d "$message" \
      "$server/$topic" &
    disown
    # Log
    echo "$(date -Iseconds) [$priority] $title: $message" >> "$HOME/.acfs/logs/notifications.log"
  }

### Integration Points (where to call acfs_notify)

1. Agent session completion hooks:
   - Claude Code: Add a Notification hook in ~/.claude/settings.json (or per-project .claude/settings.json)
     Claude Code fires "Notification" hook when it produces a notification. Wire this to acfs_notify.
   - Codex: Codex CLI emits exit codes -- wrap the cod alias to call acfs_notify on exit
   - Gemini: Same wrapping approach for gmi alias

2. ACFS install/update completion:
   - install.sh: Add acfs_notify call after webhook_notify (line ~5590)
   - scripts/lib/update.sh: Add notify at end of print_summary

3. acfs doctor failures:
   - scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Add notify on non-zero exit

4. Nightly update:
   - scripts/lib/nightly_update.sh: Add notify at completion

5. GH Actions CI failure:
   - This requires a webhook receiver or polling -- defer to v2
   - For now, document how users can add ntfy.sh to their own GH Actions workflows

### Agent Alias Wrapping (for session end notifications)

Modify the cc, cod, gmi aliases in acfs.zshrc to wrap agent invocations:

Current (line ~456): alias cc='claude'
New:
  cc() {
    claude "$@"
    local exit_code=$?
    if command -v acfs_notify &>/dev/null; then
      if [ $exit_code -eq 0 ]; then
        acfs_notify "Claude Code" "Session completed in $(basename $PWD)" "default" "white_check_mark"
      else
        acfs_notify "Claude Code" "Session exited with code $exit_code in $(basename $PWD)" "high" "warning"
      fi
    fi
    return $exit_code
  }

## Files to Modify/Create

Modify:
- acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc (add notifications subcommand routing + agent alias wrapping)
- scripts/completions/acfs.bash (add notifications completions)
- scripts/completions/_acfs (add notifications zsh completions)

Create:
- scripts/lib/notifications.sh (subcommand handler: enable/disable/test/status/topic/events/set-server)
- scripts/lib/notify.sh (notification library: acfs_notify function + quiet hours + logging)

Optionally modify:
- install.sh (add acfs_notify call after webhook_notify at install completion)
- scripts/lib/update.sh (add notify at update completion)
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh (add notify on failure)
- scripts/lib/nightly_update.sh (add notify at completion)

## Testing Plan

### Unit Tests (tests/unit/notifications.bats)
Using bats-core framework:
- test_notify_disabled_by_default: verify acfs_notify is a no-op when notifications.enabled is false
- test_notify_enabled_sends_curl: mock curl, verify it's called with correct args when enabled
- test_notify_topic_generation: verify topic format matches acfs-{hostname}-{hex8}
- test_notify_quiet_hours_respected: set quiet hours, verify no curl call during quiet window
- test_notify_quiet_hours_outside: verify notifications send outside quiet window
- test_notify_missing_topic: verify graceful no-op when topic is empty
- test_notify_missing_config: verify graceful no-op when config file doesn't exist
- test_notify_https_only: verify non-HTTPS server URLs are rejected
- test_notify_logging: verify notification is appended to log file
- test_notify_priority_mapping: verify priority parameter is passed through correctly
- test_notify_tags: verify tags header is set when tags are provided, absent when not

### Unit Tests (tests/unit/notifications_subcommand.bats)
- test_notifications_enable: verify config file is created/updated with correct keys
- test_notifications_disable: verify enabled is set to false
- test_notifications_test: verify test notification is sent
- test_notifications_status: verify current config is displayed
- test_notifications_topic: verify topic URL is printed
- test_notifications_set_server: verify server URL is updated in config
- test_notifications_idempotent_enable: verify enabling twice doesn't change topic

### E2E Test Script (new: scripts/e2e/test_notifications.sh)
Comprehensive end-to-end test:
1. Start with clean state (no notifications config)
2. Run acfs notifications status -- verify shows disabled
3. Run acfs notifications enable -- verify config created, topic generated
4. Parse topic from output
5. Start a background subscriber: curl -s ntfy.sh/$TOPIC/json &
6. Run acfs notifications test -- verify subscriber receives notification
7. Verify log file created at ~/.acfs/logs/notifications.log
8. Verify log entry matches sent notification
9. Run acfs notifications disable -- verify config updated
10. Run acfs notifications test -- verify no notification sent (disabled)
11. Re-enable, set quiet hours to current time window
12. Run acfs notifications test -- verify no notification sent (quiet hours)
13. Test agent wrapper: run cc --version (fast), verify notification fires
14. Test with self-hosted server URL: acfs notifications set-server https://custom.ntfy.example
15. Verify config updated with new server
16. All output logged with timestamps to scripts/e2e/test_notifications.log
17. Each test prints [PASS] or [FAIL] with descriptive message
18. Final summary: X/Y tests passed

### Integration Tests
- Run existing test suite: bats tests/unit/**/*.bats (must not regress)
- ShellCheck on new scripts: shellcheck scripts/lib/notify.sh scripts/lib/notifications.sh
- Lint checks: scripts/tests/lint_*.sh must pass on new files

## Edge Cases
- yq not installed: fall back to grep/sed for YAML parsing, or check for yq in enable flow
- Config file doesn't exist: create it on enable, no-op otherwise
- Network unreachable: curl -sf with timeout handles this (non-blocking, disowned)
- ntfy.sh public server rate limits: document in help, suggest self-hosted for heavy use
- Multiple VPS instances: each gets its own topic (hostname in topic name ensures uniqueness)
- Agent crashes (SIGKILL): the wrapper function won't run -- this is acceptable, only clean exits notify
- Concurrent notifications: each curl is independent, no race conditions
- Log file growth: append-only, users can rotate/truncate as needed
- Existing webhook.sh: notifications complement webhooks, don't replace them. Both can be active.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:21:36.447367448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:08:22.113696958Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

- test_notify_enabled_sends_curl: mock curl, verify it's called with correct args when enabled
- test_notify_topic_generation: verify topic format matches acfs-{hostname}-{hex8}
- test_notify_quiet_hours_respected: set quiet hours, verify no curl call during quiet window
- test_notify_quiet_hours_outside: verify notifications send outside quiet window
- test_notify_missing_topic: verify graceful no-op when topic is empty
- test_notify_missing_config: verify graceful no-op when config file doesn't exist
- test_notify_https_only: verify non-HTTPS server URLs are rejected
- test_notify_logging: verify notification is appended to log file
- test_notify_priority_mapping: verify priority parameter is passed through correctly
- test_notify_tags: verify tags header is set when tags are provided, absent when not

### Unit Tests (tests/unit/notifications_subcommand.bats)
- test_notifications_enable: verify config file is created/updated with correct keys
- test_notifications_disable: verify enabled is set to false
- test_notifications_test: verify test notification is sent
- test_notifications_status: verify current config is displayed
- test_notifications_topic: verify topic URL is printed
- test_notifications_set_server: verify server URL is updated in config
- test_notifications_idempotent_enable: verify enabling twice doesn't change topic

### E2E Test Script (new: scripts/e2e/test_notifications.sh)
Comprehensive end-to-end test:
1. Start with clean state (no notifications config)
2. Run acfs notifications status -- verify shows disabled
3. Run acfs notifications enable -- verify config created, topic generated
4. Parse topic from output
5. Start a background subscriber: curl -s ntfy.sh/$TOPIC/json &
6. Run acfs notifications test -- verify subscriber receives notification
7. Verify log file created at ~/.acfs/logs/notifications.log
8. Verify log entry matches sent notification
9. Run acfs notifications disable -- verify config updated
10. Run acfs notifications test -- verify no notification sent (disabled)
11. Re-enable, set quiet hours to current time window
12. Run acfs notifications test -- verify no notification sent (quiet hours)
13. Test agent wrapper: run cc --version (fast), verify notification fires
14. Test with self-hosted server URL: acfs notifications set-server https://custom.ntfy.example
15. Verify config updated with new server
16. All output logged with timestamps to scripts/e2e/test_notifications.log
17. Each test prints [PASS] or [FAIL] with descriptive message
18. Final summary: X/Y tests passed

### Integration Tests
- Run existing test suite: bats tests/unit/**/*.bats (must not regress)
- ShellCheck on new scripts: shellcheck scripts/lib/notify.sh scripts/lib/notifications.sh
- Lint checks: scripts/tests/lint_*.sh must pass on new files

## Edge Cases
- yq not installed: fall back to grep/sed for YAML parsing, or check for yq in enable flow
- Config file doesn't exist: create it on enable, no-op otherwise
- Network unreachable: curl -sf with timeout handles this (non-blocking, disowned)
- ntfy.sh public server rate limits: document in help, suggest self-hosted for heavy use
- Multiple VPS instances: each gets its own topic (hostname in topic name ensures uniqueness)
- Agent crashes (SIGKILL): the wrapper function won't run -- this is acceptable, only clean exits notify
- Concurrent notifications: each curl is independent, no race conditions
- Log file growth: append-only, users can rotate/truncate as needed
- Existing webhook.sh: notifications complement webhooks, don't replace them.
